global.description: Reserved for platform-injected globals (e.g. Rancher's global.cattle.*, ArgoCD, Flux). Not consumed by this chart.

preset.description: Preset for the cluster type. 'standard' (default) uses a hostPath DaemonSet for logs. 'gke-autopilot' and 'eks-fargate' switch to API-mode log collection with a hardened security context.

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in generated resource names.

fullnameOverride.description: Override the fully qualified resource name. Useful for installing side-by-side with an existing release that shares the default cluster-scoped resource names.

url.description: URL of your OneUptime instance (e.g., https://oneuptime.example.com)

apiKey.description: Project API key from OneUptime (Settings > API Keys)

labels.description: Project Labels auto-attached to every record. Each key/value becomes an `oneuptime.label.<key>=<value>` resource attribute, which the OneUptime ingest pipeline promotes to a project Label.

clusterName.description: Unique name for this cluster (used as k8s.cluster.name attribute)

namespaceFilters.description: Namespace filters to limit which namespaces are monitored

include.description: If set, only these namespaces are monitored (empty = all namespaces)

debug.description: Debug sidecar — injects a shell + network tools into the metrics Deployment and logs DaemonSet for kubectl-exec troubleshooting (the collector images are distroless and have no shell). Off by default.

shareProcessNamespace.description: Share the pod PID namespace so the sidecar can inspect the collector process.

command.description: Keep-alive command for the idle sidecar (default: sleep infinity).

securityContext.description: securityContext for the sidecar. Add NET_RAW/NET_ADMIN capabilities here for ping/traceroute/tcpdump.

mode.description: Advanced override. Leave empty to let 'preset' decide. daemonset: hostPath filelog DaemonSet. api: Kubernetes API log tailer Deployment (GKE Autopilot compatible). disabled: no log collection.

api.description: Configuration for api-mode log tailer (Kubernetes API-based, GKE Autopilot compatible)

resourceSpecs.description: Pull full K8s resource specs for dashboard detail views (labels, annotations, env vars, status, etc.)

enabled.description: Enable pulling full resource specs via k8sobjects receiver

interval.description: How often to pull resource specs (e.g., 300s, 5m)

vpa.description: Enable VPA collection (requires VPA CRDs installed in the cluster)

kubeletstats.description: Extra metrics from the kubelet that aren't on by default in the OTel kubeletstats receiver. Both groups run inside the existing DaemonSet collector, no new pods.

utilizationMetrics.description: Saturation metrics: CPU/memory as a % of pod request and limit. Six extra metric families per existing series; harmless when pods have no request/limit set.

volumeMetrics.description: Per-PVC disk usage (k8s.volume.available / capacity). One series per PVC per pod — bounded on most clusters, can be heavy on stateful workloads with thousands of PVCs.

cadvisor.description: Scrape the kubelet's /metrics/cadvisor endpoint for CFS throttling and OOM kill counters that the kubeletstats receiver doesn't translate. Allowlist keeps only the listed metric names so collector memory stays bounded.

metricsAllowlist.description: Exact __name__ allowlist. Each entry adds one series per container, so keep it tight.

kubeStateMetrics.description: Cluster-state metrics from kube-state-metrics. Off by default because the bundled mode adds a Deployment to the chart's footprint.

mode.description: 'bundled' deploys a small KSM Deployment+Service. 'external' only adds a Prometheus scrape; you must set `endpoint`.

endpoint.description: URL to scrape when mode == 'external'. Example: http://kube-state-metrics.kube-system.svc.cluster.local:8080/metrics

metricsAllowlist.description: Exact __name__ allowlist. KSM exports ~100 metric families by default; only keep the ones that power monitors.

ebpf.description: OpenTelemetry eBPF Instrumentation (OBI) — auto-captures HTTP/gRPC traces and request metrics from every pod on the node, no SDK required. Enabled by default. Requires Linux kernel 5.8+ and the ability to run privileged pods.

enabled.description: Enable the OpenTelemetry eBPF Instrumentation DaemonSet. Default: true.

autoTargetExe.description: Glob pattern of executable paths to auto-instrument (e.g., '*' for all, '*/python,*/java' to scope).

excludeExePaths.description: Comma-separated glob list of executable paths to skip.

printTraces.description: Print spans to OBI pod stdout in addition to OTLP export. Useful for verifying traffic capture during install.

features.description: Granular toggles for the metric families OBI extracts from the captured traffic. Each is independent — disabling one drops that family but leaves traces and other families intact.

spanMetrics.description: Per-span metrics keyed on span attributes (OBI `application_span`).

serviceGraph.description: Service graph edge metrics, drives the service map (OBI `application_service_graph`).

networkMetrics.description: Pod-to-pod TCP/UDP flow bytes and packets (OBI `network`).

networkInterZoneMetrics.description: Inter-zone variant of network metrics. Doubles cardinality; off by default (OBI `network_inter_zone`).

tcpStats.description: Node-level TCP stats (RTT, failed connections, retransmits) (OBI `stats`).

contextPropagation.description: Inject W3C `traceparent` into outbound traffic so traces span services without SDK changes.

contextPropagationMode.description: How OBI injects the traceparent. 'headers' (default, safe with service meshes / mTLS / eBPF CNIs) — HTTP/1.1 request headers only. 'ip' — IPv4/IPv6 option on outbound packets (covers raw TCP but may be dropped by middleboxes). 'all' — both (most coverage; the IP-option half can break service-mesh proxies).

trackRequestHeaders.description: Track HTTP request headers in kernel for traceparent extraction. Only effective if `contextPropagation` is true.

logToTraceCorrelation.description: Inject trace_id / span_id into JSON log lines from instrumented processes (via OBI's log enricher) and lift them onto the LogRecord in the filelog pipeline so trace ↔ log click-through works. Non-JSON logs pass through unchanged. **Off by default** — the log enricher rewrites the application's stdout buffer in-process and is incompatible with LD_PRELOAD-based APM agents (Dynatrace OneAgent, New Relic, AppDynamics, Datadog, Instana); enabling it inside a process that already has one of those agents typically crashes the application with SIGSEGV. Opt in only when no LD_PRELOAD APM agent runs in the same pods (or scope `logEnricher.services` to skip those pods).

logEnricher.description: Log enricher process selector. Only consulted when `logToTraceCorrelation` is true. Each entry under `services` matches one or more processes; OBI enables the in-process write() uprobe and buffer rewrite on every matched process. Default `exe_path: '*'` matches everything OBI auto-discovers. Narrow it (by `languages`, `k8s_pod_labels`, etc.) to skip pods running an LD_PRELOAD-based APM agent.

services.description: List of OBI log_enricher service selectors. Rendered verbatim into obi-config.yaml. Each entry is a GlobAttributes selector with fields like exe_path, languages, k8s_pod_labels, k8s_pod_annotations, open_ports, cmd_args.

profiling.description: Continuous CPU profiling via OpenTelemetry eBPF Profiler. Runs as a DaemonSet, samples stacks across all languages without SDKs, ships OTLP profiles directly to OneUptime. Default: enabled.

hostMetrics.description: Per-node OS metrics (disk I/O, inodes, NIC errors, load average, paging) via the OTel hostmetrics receiver.

auditLogs.description: Kubernetes API server audit logs. Off by default; managed K8s usually doesn't expose them as files.

csi.description: Scrape Prometheus metrics from CSI (storage) driver pods. Off by default.

coreDns.description: Scrape Prometheus metrics from the cluster CoreDNS service. Off by default.

serviceMesh.description: Service mesh observability (Istio / Linkerd sidecar metrics)
